text: β-alanine supplementation improves exercise outcomes with a median effect size of 0.374 compared to 0.108 for placebo.
β-alanine supplementation improves exercise capacity but not exercise performance in controlled trials.
β-alanine supplementation significantly improves exercise lasting 60-240 seconds, consistent with its role in H+ buffering during high-intensity efforts.
β-alanine supplementation also improves exercise lasting longer than 240 seconds, though with a smaller effect than the 60-240 second range.
β-alanine supplementation provides no benefit for exercise lasting less than 60 seconds.
The median improvement from β-alanine supplementation is 2.85% in exercise outcomes when a median total dose of 179 g is consumed over the supplementation period.
β-alanine acts as a substrate for carnosine synthesis, which is a major contributor to intracellular H+ buffering during high-intensity exercise.
